title: 'It' Director Andy Muschietti on Adapting Stephen King
format: Sean Fennessey talks 'It' twice: first rereading the Stephen King novel with Kate Nibbs, then with director Andy Muschietti on his adaptation, building Pennywise, and the email King sent after watching it.

segments.heading: Rereading 'It' With Kate Nibbs
segments.note: the book, the miniseries, the new movie

films.title: It
films.note: Fennessey calls the new adaptation scary and sweet in equal measure, and great besides. Finn Wolfhard plays Richie; unlike Seth Green's version in the 1990 miniseries, he drops the book's racist voices.

films.title: It
films.note: Muschietti isolates the losers as kids and wants a second half for their adult lives. King watched the DCP alone in Florida and emailed back: 'It's brilliant.'

films.title: Mama
films.note: Grew out of a three-minute short that spread on YouTube; Guillermo del Toro called and offered to produce the feature.

films.title: Close Encounters of the Third Kind
films.note: This is what started it. He saw it at five at a Buenos Aires drive-in, and the alien reveal fused magic and horror for him.

films.title: The Omega Man
films.note: One of the Saturday-night TV horror films he says made the biggest early impression on him.

films.title: The Manster
films.note: Another title from the childhood horror show hosted by a Spanish Lon Chaney.

films.title: The Abominable Dr. Phibes
films.note: The Vincent Price film from that same show that blew his mind.

films.title: Trilogy of Terror
films.note: The Zuni-doll segment terrified him; a shared reference with del Toro.

films.title: The Shining
films.note: Raised in the adaptation talk: King has been vocal about his displeasure with Kubrick's version.

films.title: Dunkirk
films.note: His pick for the last great thing he saw. A visceral experience whose puzzle structure covers for a simple story.

Close Encounters of the Third Kind.title: Close Encounters of the Third Kind
